dynamics: Shaan drives research-backed ideas and rules-changes lens; Sam extends with storytelling and riffs on mechanics. Both co-equal, high energy, rapid-fire idea discussion. Editor Dan provides occasional meta-commentary and closes with rating. Lots of tangential explorations into phone discipline and payment psychology.
frameworks: Unbundle existing platforms to create user-side value
Identify problem, then sell solution back to institutions
College is underutilized playground for hustlers
Reassert agency: determine who pays whom to reset power dynamic
learnings: Business opportunities emerge when regulatory inflection points open new legal pathways
Unethical businesses teach useful business-model mechanics worth analyzing separately from ethics
Simple adoption beats feature-rich in emerging compliance markets
Compliance software becomes system-of-record once schools adopt, creating defensible moat
Ad networks fragment value; users currently receive zero cut from traffic they generate
Network effects from same-dorm relationships can bootstrap agent businesses in sports

banger_quotes.quote: I installed this phone for my convenience, not their convenience

banger_quotes.text: There's some kid sitting on Alabama's campus who's going to be worth $50 million

banger_quotes.quote: Facebook catches all the value. Google catches all the value. You get none.
Diagnose the problem, then sell them the prescription

notable: Unusual meta-moment where host Sam confronts editor Dan on-mic about who controls the podcast schedule, using payment direction as philosophical reset tool. Episode spans 10+ disparate ideas without traditional segmentation.
